{"step_by_step_thinking": "The pharyngotympanic tube, also known as the Eustachian tube, is supplied by several arteries. From the given documents, we can see that the ascending pharyngeal artery, ascending palatine artery, and the artery of the pterygoid canal all supply the Eustachian tube. However, the middle meningeal artery does not supply the Eustachian tube. Therefore, the middle meningeal artery is the exception.", "answer_choice": "C"}
